summary: The quiz lifecycle ignores the configured open_at and due_at values when students start attempts, submit answers, or finalize attempts. Any enrolled student can therefore take or continue a quiz outside the intended availability window, undermining assessment integrity.

evidence_snippets.code:
title = request.form.get('title', '').strip()
description = request.form.get('description', '').strip()
open_at = request.form.get('open_at', '').strip()
due_at = request.form.get('due_at', '').strip()
...
item = create_quiz(course_id, get_current_user()['user_id'], title, description, open_at, due_at)
evidence_snippets.reason: The quiz management logic accepts and stores open_at and due_at as meaningful scheduling controls.
Students can start attempts without any check against the quiz's open_at or due_at values.
Answer submission and final submission likewise occur with no server-side validation that the quiz is currently open or not past due.
The repository persists scheduling fields, confirming that the application models time-bounded quiz availability but does not enforce it on attempt actions.

root_cause: The attempt lifecycle enforces only coarse role checks and never validates quiz availability rules against open_at and due_at before allowing start, answer, or submission actions.

patch_locations.function_name: start_attempt_submit / api_start_attempt / answer_question_submit / api_answer_question / submit_attempt_submit / api_submit_attempt

repair_plan: Before creating an attempt, recording an answer, or accepting final submission, load the target quiz and enforce a consistent availability policy using open_at and due_at. Reject actions before the quiz opens and after it closes, and apply the same rule to both HTML and JSON endpoints so students cannot bypass scheduling constraints through alternate interfaces.
